action.thought_summary: Starting with interaction category to understand core messaging behavior - whether this is 1-on-1 only or includes group chats, and what message types are needed.
action.question: Will users communicate only in one-on-one conversations, or do you need group chat functionality as well? Also, what types of messages should be supported beyond text (e.g., images, files, voice messages)?

action.thought_summary: Turn 1 covered interaction (group chat, adding friends) and content (message types). Turn 2 should prioritize style/UI as per guidelines. I'll ask about visual/theme preferences for the messaging app.
action.question: Do you have any preferences for the app's visual style, such as a light or dark theme, specific color scheme, or a particular messaging layout (e.g., bubble-style chat vs. minimal flat design)?

action.thought_summary: Covered: group chat, friend management, message types (interaction/content), and color scheme (style). Still missing: key interaction behaviors like message status indicators, editing/deleting, and notifications. Turn 3 of 3, so this is the final question before finishing.
action.question: Should users be able to see when their messages have been delivered or read (e.g., read receipts, delivery indicators), and do they need the ability to edit or delete sent messages?
